blocks:
Surah Al-Fajr

Meccan, and it consists of thirty-two verses according to the Hijazi count. Thirty according to the Kufi and Shami counts, and twenty-nine according to the Basri count. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالْفَجْرِ. (1) (By the Dawn) (٢) وَلَيالٍ عَشْرٍ ** (2) (and by the ten nights) Allah swore by the bursting forth of the morning. Al-Qummi said: There is no waw (واو) in it, rather it is ** الْفَجْرِ وَلَيالٍ عَشْرٍ ** (By the Dawn and by the ten nights). He said: [These are] the ten [nights] of Dhul-Hijjah (ذي الحجّة). (٣) وَالشَّفْعِ وَالْوَتْرِ ** (3) (and by the even and the odd) And it was recited with fath (بالفتح) [on the shīn and wāw]. It was said: meaning all things, their even (شفعها) and their odd (وترها). And Al-Qummi said: الشَّفْعِ ** (the even) means two rak'ahs (ركعتان), and وَالْوَتْرِ ** (the odd) means one rak'ah (ركعة). He said: And in another narration, he said: الشَّفْعِ ** (the even) means Al-Hasan and Al-Husayn (peace be upon them both), and وَالْوَتْرِ ** (the odd) means the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And in al-Majma', narrated from Al-Baqir and Al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): الشَّفْعِ ** (the even) means the Day of al-Tarwiyah (يوم التّروية), and وَالْوَتْرِ ** (the odd) means the Day of 'Arafah (يوم عرفة).

(٤) وَاللَّيْلِ إِذا يَسْرِ ** (4) and by the night when it departs, It is said: when it passes, like His saying: وَاللَّيْلِ إِذْ أَدْبَرَ ** (and by the night when it retreats). Al-Qummi said: It is the night of Jam' (ليلة جمع). (٥) هَلْ فِي ذلِكَ قَسَمٌ لِذِي حِجْرٍ ** (5) Is there an oath in that for a person of intellect? who considers it. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: the one who possesses intellect (عقل). And the object of the oath (المقسم عليه) is omitted, meaning: they shall surely be punished (ليعذّبنّ), as is indicated by what follows it. (٦) أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِعادٍ ** (6) Have you not regarded how your Lord dealt with ‘Ad, Meaning the descendants of 'Ad son of 'Aws son of Iram son of Sam son of Noah (peace be upon him), the people of Hud. They were named after their ancestor, as has been said. (٧) إِرَمَ ** (7) Iram, It is an appositional phrase (عطف بيان) for 'Ad, based on the estimation of an omitted annex (مضاف), meaning: the tribe (سبط) of Iram and the people (أهل) of Iram. ذاتِ الْعِمادِ (the city of the pillars,) Possessing the lofty structure (البناء الرّفيع) or the tall stature (القدود الطّوال). (٨) الَّتِي لَمْ يُخْلَقْ مِثْلُها فِي الْبِلادِ ** (8) the like of which was not created in the cities? It is said that 'Ad had two sons, Shaddad and Shadid. They ruled and conquered, then Shadid died, and the authority (الامر) was secured for Shaddad. He ruled the inhabited world (المعمورة), and its kings submitted to him. He heard mention of Paradise (الجنّة), so he built a paradise (جنّة) resembling it in some of the deserts (صحارى) of 'Adan and named it Iram. When it was completed, he traveled to it with his people. When he was a distance of one day and one night's journey away from it, Allah sent against them a cry (صيحة) from the heaven, and they perished.

(٩) وَثَمُودَ الَّذِينَ جابُوا الصَّخْرَ ** (9) and Thamūd, who hewed out the rocks They cut it and took it as dwellings, due to His saying: وَتَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ الْجِبالِ بُيُوتاً بِالْوادِ (and you hew out houses from the mountains in the valley) (وادي القرى).

(١٠) وَفِرْعَوْنَ ذِي الْأَوْتادِ ** (10) and Pharaoh, the holder of the stakes? The reason for naming him the holder of the stakes (ذِي الْأَوْتادِ) has passed in Sūrat Ṣād.

(١١) الَّذِينَ طَغَوْا فِي الْبِلادِ. (11) Those who were rebellious in the cities,

(١٢) فَأَكْثَرُوا فِيهَا الْفَسادَ ** (12) and caused much corruption in them. Through disbelief (الكفر) and oppression (الظلم).

(١٣) فَصَبَّ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّكَ سَوْطَ عَذابٍ. (13) So your Lord poured upon them the scourge of a punishment.

(١٤) إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَبِالْمِرْصادِ ** (14) Indeed your Lord is in ambush. The place where the observer (الرّصد) waits in anticipation.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Its meaning is: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ ** (Indeed your Lord) is capable of recompensing the people of disobedience (اهل المعاصي) with their recompense.

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Al-Mirṣād (المرصاد) is a bridge (قنطرة) over the Path (الصراط). No servant who has wronged another servant shall pass over it. And another narrative concerning it will follow.

(١٥) فَأَمَّا الْإِنْسانُ إِذا مَا ابْتَلاهُ رَبُّهُ ** (15) As for man, when his Lord tests him, He tests him with wealth (الغنى) and ease (اليُسر), so He honors him and grants him comfort (وَنَعَّمَهُ) through status (بالجاه) and property (والمال), فَأَكْرَمَهُ وَنَعَّمَهُ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّي أَكْرَمَنِ. and is bountiful toward him, he says, 'My Lord has honored me.' (١٦) وَأَمَّا إِذا مَا ابْتَلاهُ ** (16) But when He tests him, He tests him with poverty (بالفقر) and restriction (والتقتير), فَقَدَرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقَهُ ** and tightens His provision for him, He restricts (فضيّق) and limits (وقتر) it for him. ** فَيَقُولُ رَبِّي أَهانَنِ ** he says, 'My Lord has humiliated me.' [He says this] due to the shortness of his sight (لقصور نظره) and the badness of his thought (وسوء فكره), for restriction (التقتير) may lead to the honor (كرامة) of both abodes (الدّارين), while expansion (التوسعة) may lead to the aim of enemies (قصد الأعداء) and immersion (الانهماك) in the love of the world (حبّ الدنيا). For this reason, He censured him for both his sayings and deterred him [with the word] كَلَّا (Nay!). And أَكْرَمَنِ and أَهانَنِ were read without the letter yā’ (ياء), and with tashdīd (التشديد) in قَدَّرَ**.

(١٧) كَلَّا بَلْ لا تُكْرِمُونَ الْيَتِيمَ. (17) No indeed! But you do not honor the orphan, **(١٨) وَلا تَحَاضُّونَ عَلى طَعامِ الْمِسْكِينِ ** (18) nor do you urge one another to feed the needy, Meaning, rather, their action is worse than their saying, and it is more indicative of their covetousness for wealth, namely: that they do not honor the orphan by looking after him and showing kindness, and by making him independent of the humiliation of begging, nor do they urge their own families to feed the needy, much less others.

(١٩) ** وَتَأْكُلُونَ التُّراثَ (19) and you consume the inheritance) the inheritance (الميراث) ** أَكْلاً لَمًّا ** (with an indiscriminate consumption) possessing لمّ (gathering), meaning: mixing between the lawful (الحلال) and the unlawful (الحرام), for they used not to allow women and children to inherit, and they would consume their shares, or they consume what the deceased (المورّث) gathered from lawful (حلال) and unlawful (حرام) sources, while being aware of that.

(٢٠) وَتُحِبُّونَ الْمالَ حُبًّا جَمًّا ** (And you love wealth with an ardent love.) abundantly (كثيراً) along with greed (حرص) and desire (شهوة). And it was recited (وقرئ) with the tāʾ (بالتاء) in all of them, based on a shift in address (الالتفات) or the implied word (تقدير) 'Say' (قل). (٢١) ** كَلَّا ** (No indeed!) A deterrent (ردع) for them from that (behavior), and what follows it is a threat (وعيد) concerning it. ** إِذا دُكَّتِ الْأَرْضُ دَكًّا دَكًّا ** (No indeed! When the earth is pounded to powder, pounding after pounding,) pounding after pounding (بعد دكّ) until the mountains (الجبال) and hills (التلال) become low (منخفضة), or scattered dust (هباءً منبثّاً). Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: "It is the earthquake (الزّلزلة)."

(٢٢) وَجاءَ رَبُّكَ ** (22) And your Lord comes meaning the command of your Lord. Thus it is in al-Tawhid and al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), meaning the signs of His power and the effects of His subjugation (قهره) appeared. That is likened to what appears upon the presence of the sovereign (السلطان) from the effects of his awe (هيبته) and his governance (سياسته). وَالْمَلَكُ صَفًّا صَفًّا ** and the angels, rank upon rank according to their stations (منازلهم) and their ranks (مراتبهم).

(٢٣) وَجِيءَ يَوْمَئِذٍ بِجَهَنَّمَ (23) And on that day Gehenna will be brought. like His saying: وَبُرِّزَتِ الْجَحِيمُ. (And the Hellfire is displayed.)

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that he said: When this verse was revealed: وَجِيءَ يَوْمَئِذٍ بِجَهَنَّمَ (And on that day Gehenna will be brought), the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was asked about it. He said: "The Trustworthy Spirit (الرّوح الأمين) informed me that Allah—there is no god other than Him—when He displays the creatures and gathers the first and the last, Gehenna will be brought, led by a thousand reins (زِمَام). A hundred thousand [angels] of the harsh and severe ones (الغلاظ الشداد) hold every rein, leading it. It has a crash (هدّة), and wrath, and roaring (زفير), and gasping (شهيق). And indeed, it lets out a roar (زفرة); were it not that Allah postponed them for the Reckoning, it would destroy everyone.

"Then a neck (عنق) will emerge from it, encompassing the creatures, the righteous (البرّ) among them and the wicked (الفاجر). Allah has not created any servant among the servants of Allah, neither an angel nor a prophet, except that he cries out: 'My Lord, myself! Myself!' And you, O Prophet of Allah, will cry out: 'My community! My community!'

"Then the Path (الصراط) will be placed over it, finer than a hair and sharper than the edge of a sword. Upon it are three bridges (قناطر). As for the first, upon it is Trustworthiness (الأمانة) and Kinship (الرّحم). The second, upon it is Prayer (الصلاة). And the third, upon it is the Lord of the worlds, there is no god other than Him.

"So they will be tasked with passing over it. Kinship (الرّحم) and Trustworthiness (الأمانة) will hold them accountable. If they are saved from that, Prayer (الصلاة) will detain them. If they are saved from that, the final destination will be to the Lord of the worlds. And that is His saying: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَبِالْمِرْصادِ (Indeed your Lord is in ambush.)

"And the people upon the Path (الصراط) are clinging with a hand, and a foot slips, and they hold fast with a foot. And the angels around it cry out: 'O Forbearing One (يا حليم), pardon and overlook, and return with Your favor (فضلك). Grant safety! Grant safety!' And the people are falling into the Fire like moths (كالفراش) into it.

"Then, when a survivor is saved by the mercy of Allah, he passes over it and says: 'All praise belongs to Allah, and by His favor (نعمته) good deeds (الصالحات) are perfected and virtues (الحسنات) are purified. And all praise belongs to Allah, Who saved me from you after despair, through His grace (منّه) and His favor (فضله). Indeed, our Lord is All-forgiving, All-thankful.'"

And in al-Kafi: [A narration] similar in meaning [to the preceding text]. يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَذَكَّرُ الْإِنْسانُ وَأَنَّى لَهُ الذِّكْرَى (On that day man will remember, but what good will the remembrance do him?) [meaning] benefit for the remembrance (الذكرى).

(٢٤) يَقُولُ يا لَيْتَنِي قَدَّمْتُ لِحَياتِي ** (He will say, 'Alas! Had I sent ahead [some good] for my life!') meaning, for this life of mine, or during the time of my life in the world, [I wish I had sent ahead] righteous deeds (أعمالاً صالحة). (٢٥) فَيَوْمَئِذٍ لا يُعَذِّبُ عَذابَهُ أَحَدٌ ** (So on that day no one will punish as He punishes,) meaning, like His punishment. (٢٦) وَلا يُوثِقُ وَثاقَهُ أَحَدٌ ** (and no one will bind as He binds.) meaning, like His binding, due to his extreme disbelief (كفره) and obstinacy (عناده). And al-Qummi said that it (the pronoun) refers to the second [meaning, i.e., the disbeliever]. And it was recited (وقرئ) based on the passive voice (على بناء المفعول) in both [verses]. And in al-Majma', he narrated it from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and this [reading/interpretation] is better, due to the difficulty (التكلّف) in interpreting the first [reading, the active voice] by having to estimate [the phrase] 'except Allah' (إلّا الله) or something else.

يا أَيَّتُهَا النَّفْسُ الْمُطْمَئِنَّةُ (٢٧) (O soul at rest!) (This phrase is understood) upon the intention of the saying (على إرادة القول), and it is the soul that has found rest (اطمأنّت) in the Truth (الحقّ). ارْجِعِي إِلى رَبِّكِ (٢٨) راضِيَةً مَرْضِيَّةً. (Return to your Lord, pleased and pleasing.) Return to your Lord, just as you originated from Him, راضِيَةً مَرْضِيَّةً. فَادْخُلِي فِي عِبادِي (٢٩) وَادْخُلِي جَنَّتِي. (Enter among My servants,) ** فَادْخُلِي فِي عِبادِي **(١) and enter My Paradise.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked whether the believer is compelled (يُكْرَهُ) when his soul is seized. He said: "No, by Allah. When the Angel of Death comes to seize his soul, he becomes distressed at that moment. The Angel of Death says to him: 'O friend of Allah (يا وليّ الله), do not be distressed. By Him Who sent Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, I am kinder (أَبَرّ) to you and more compassionate toward you than a merciful parent would be if he were present with you. Open your eyes and look.'" He (the Imam) said: "And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Commander of the Faithful, Fatimah, al-Hasan, al-Husayn, and the Imams (peace be upon them) are manifested for him. It is said to him: 'This is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Commander of the Faithful, Fatimah, al-Hasan, al-Husayn, and the Imams (peace be upon them)—they are your companions.' So he opens his eyes and looks. Then a caller from the Lord of Might (ربّ العزّة) calls out to his soul, saying: يا أَيَّتُهَا النَّفْسُ الْمُطْمَئِنَّةُ (O soul at rest!)—at rest in Muhammad and his Household (peace be upon them)—ارْجِعِي إِلى رَبِّكِ (Return to your Lord), راضِيَةً (pleased) with the Wilayah (الولاية), مَرْضِيَّةً ** (pleasing) with the reward (الثواب), فَادْخُلِي فِي عِبادِي ** (Enter among My servants)—meaning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his Household (peace be upon them)—**وَادْخُلِي جَنَّتِي ** (and enter My Paradise)." There is nothing dearer to him than the extraction of his soul and joining the caller.

And al-Qummi mentioned its meaning concisely. And narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: It means al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon them).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Recite Surah al-Fajr in your obligatory prayers (فرائضكم) and your supererogatory prayers (نوافلكم), for it is the Surah of al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace and blessings be upon them both). Whoever recites it will be with al-Husayn (peace be upon him) on the Day of Resurrection in a rank of Paradise."

footnote (١) Meaning: among the company (*زمرة*) of My righteous, chosen servants (*عبادي الصالحين المصطفين*) with whom I am pleased.
